audit_text: [HIGH] tests/test_daily_probe.py is missing. diff_probe and cutover_smoke both have full test suites. daily_probe.py has the same pure-function decomposition (run_probes, write_log, render_human, _result_to_jsonl) and zero test coverage — it runs daily and has no regression net.
spec_anchor: scripts/daily_probe.py module docstring (lines 1-54) + pure-function surface lines 229,243,258,295
code_paths_checked: tests/ directory listing (~95 test_*.py files; no test_daily_probe.py)
verdict_evidence: `Glob tests/test_*probe*.py` returns only `tests/test_diff_probe.py`. `ls tests/test_daily_probe.py` returns 'No such file or directory'. `git log --all -- tests/test_daily_probe.py` is empty (file never existed). The four pure functions named in the audit are present and unit-testable in scripts/daily_probe.py at the cited line numbers.
acceptance_criteria: File tests/test_daily_probe.py exists and is collected by pytest.

Tests cover run_probes() against a stub HTTP layer (monkeypatched _execute_probe or urllib.request.urlopen) for at least: all-pass case, partial-fail case, URLError/timeout case, accepted-multi-status case (e.g. 404 on /api/quota/current passing).

Tests cover _result_to_jsonl() output shape (keys: ts, probe, path, status, accepted, elapsed_ms, passed, detail) and write_log() append semantics (re-running on the same day appends, does not truncate; SUMMARY trailer present; log_dir created mode 0700).
Tests cover render_human() empty-input branch and PASS/FAIL marker formatting.
Coverage parity with tests/test_diff_probe.py — tests don't depend on a live bearings-v1.service.
All tests pass under `uv run pytest tests/test_daily_probe.py -q`.
executor_brief: Mirror tests/test_diff_probe.py for daily_probe.py. The module exposes run_probes, write_log, render_human, _result_to_jsonl as the pure-function surface. Stub urllib.request.urlopen via monkeypatch (or pass an injected _execute_probe) to drive PASS/FAIL/URLError/multi-status branches. Use tmp_path for write_log so the test never touches ~/.local/share. The 200-or-404 PASS branch on /api/quota/current is the most spec-relevant case to lock in.

audit_text: [MEDIUM] docs/behavior/routing.md does not exist. Both daily_probe.py (line 33) and cutover_smoke.py (line 173) cross-reference "docs/behavior/routing.md §Quota guard". That file is not in docs/behavior/ (14 files, none named routing.md). Dangling ref will break lychee lint.
spec_anchor: scripts/daily_probe.py:33 + scripts/cutover_smoke.py:173 (cross-references); docs/behavior/ directory inventory (14 files)
code_paths_checked: docs/behavior/ (14 .md files: prompt-endpoint, checklists, paired-chats, bearings-cli, tool-output-streaming, vault, modals, keyboard-shortcuts, sessions, chat, memories, themes, preferences, context-menus)
docs/model-routing-v1-spec.md (does contain a 'Quota guard' anchor — wrong file though)
verdict_evidence: `Glob docs/behavior/*.md` returns 14 files, no routing.md. `ls docs/behavior/routing.md` → No such file or directory. `git log --all -- docs/behavior/routing.md` is empty (never existed). Both citations confirmed verbatim: daily_probe.py:33 reads "per ``docs/behavior/routing.md`` §\"Quota guard\"" and cutover_smoke.py:173 reads "# docs/behavior/routing.md §\"Quota guard\".". The 'Quota guard' anchor lives in docs/model-routing-v1-spec.md, not in any docs/behavior/ file.
acceptance_criteria: File docs/behavior/routing.md exists at repo root.
File contains a level-2 section heading exactly matching `## Quota guard` (or any heading whose slug resolves the §"Quota guard" anchor referenced by the scripts).

Quota-guard section documents: (a) the 'never polled → 404' branch on /api/quota/current that scripts/daily_probe.py and scripts/cutover_smoke.py rely on; (b) the QuotaPoller's role in producing snapshots; (c) what blocks routing when the bucket is exhausted (so the cross-reference is meaningful).
File scope follows the docs/behavior/ pattern: observable behavior, not implementation. Cross-link spec details to docs/model-routing-v1-spec.md instead of duplicating numeric thresholds.
`uv run pre-commit run --all-files` (which runs lychee on .md per CLAUDE.md) reports zero unresolved external/internal links pointing into docs/behavior/routing.md.
executor_brief: Create docs/behavior/routing.md with at minimum a `## Quota guard` section that documents the 200-or-404 contract the daily and cutover smoke probes depend on. Match the prose register of the existing docs/behavior/*.md files (sessions.md, chat.md are good templates). Don't restate routing-engine internals — link to docs/model-routing-v1-spec.md for spec numbers. The ship-blocker quality is the dangling cross-reference; once the anchor resolves and lychee is green, this is closed.

audit_text: [MEDIUM] diff_probe.py has no systemd service/timer. config/ only wires daily_probe.py to the 09:15 timer. diff_probe is manual-only — if continuous differential monitoring during dogfood was the intent, it needs either its own timer or an ExecStartPost= chain in the existing unit.
spec_anchor: scripts/diff_probe.py module docstring (lines 1-87) — purpose: 'run alongside scripts/daily_probe.py during the dogfood cutover window'
code_paths_checked: config/ directory listing (bearings-v1.service, bearings-v1-probe.service, bearings-v1-probe.timer, allowlists/)
config/bearings-v1-probe.service:35 (ExecStart= invokes daily_probe.py only; no ExecStartPost=)
verdict_evidence: `ls config/` shows three units total — bearings-v1.service, bearings-v1-probe.service, bearings-v1-probe.timer — none referencing diff_probe. `grep -rn 'diff_probe' config/` returns empty. The probe service's ExecStart= invokes daily_probe.py with no ExecStartPost chain (inspected lines 32-36 of bearings-v1-probe.service). The diff_probe module docstring states it 'run[s] alongside scripts/daily_probe.py during the dogfood cutover window' — there is no scheduling artifact realising that.
acceptance_criteria: Either: (a) a new pair config/bearings-v1-diff-probe.service + config/bearings-v1-diff-probe.timer exists, mirrors the hardening of bearings-v1-probe.service (NoNewPrivileges, ProtectSystem=strict, ReadWritePaths=%h/.local/share/bearings-v1/diff-probes, etc.), invokes /usr/bin/python3 scripts/diff_probe.py, fires daily on a calendar schedule (e.g. 09:20 to follow the daily probe), uses Persistent=true, and is documented in CHANGELOG.md install sequence; OR (b) bearings-v1-probe.service grows an ExecStartPost=/usr/bin/python3 scripts/diff_probe.py with ReadWritePaths extended to include diff-probes/.
`systemd-analyze verify config/bearings-v1-diff-probe.{service,timer}` (CI gate per CLAUDE.md) passes.
CHANGELOG.md install command snippet updated to enable the new timer (or note the ExecStartPost change).
Implementation chooses (a) when 'manual run on demand' is also a desired surface; chooses (b) only if diff_probe is meant to fire only when daily_probe runs.
executor_brief: Author config/bearings-v1-diff-probe.service + config/bearings-v1-diff-probe.timer modeled directly on the existing bearings-v1-probe.{service,timer} pair — same hardening flags, ReadWritePaths pointed at `~/.local/share/bearings-v1/diff-probes`, OnCalendar=09:20 (just after the 09:15 daily probe) with RandomizedDelaySec=5min and Persistent=true. Add the install/enable command to the CHANGELOG B.2 entry. systemd-analyze verify must pass.

audit_text: [LOW] No retry before FAIL. Single-shot GET with no failure_threshold. A bearings-v1.service restart overlapping the 09:15 window produces a false-positive FAIL. Standard blackbox-probe pattern is 3 attempts × short sleep before writing FAIL.
spec_anchor: scripts/daily_probe.py:157-226 (_execute_probe single-shot urlopen); scripts/daily_probe.py:94-107 (Probe dataclass — no threshold field)
code_paths_checked: scripts/daily_probe.py:157-226 (_execute_probe — single try/except around one urlopen)
scripts/daily_probe.py:229-231 (run_probes — single tuple comprehension over PROBES, no retry loop)
config/bearings-v1-probe.timer:18 (RandomizedDelaySec=5min — overlaps with potential service restart windows)
verdict_evidence: _execute_probe (daily_probe.py:157) wraps exactly one urllib.request.urlopen call (line 173); urlerror/timeout/oserror branches each return a sentinel ProbeResult immediately with no retry. The Probe dataclass has only name, path, accepted_status_codes — no failure_threshold or retry_count field. run_probes() iterates PROBES once, no outer retry loop. A connection-refused at the moment the unit fires therefore writes status_code=None, exits 1, and ends the run.
acceptance_criteria: A new module-level constant PROBE_RETRY_ATTEMPTS (default 3) and PROBE_RETRY_BACKOFF_S (default e.g. 1.0) live in daily_probe.py.

_execute_probe (or a wrapper around it) retries up to PROBE_RETRY_ATTEMPTS-1 times on URLError/TimeoutError/OSError before returning the final failure ProbeResult; an HTTPError with a status code outside accepted_status_codes is also retried (a 503 during a graceful restart is the canonical case).

An accepted status on retry attempt N produces a passed ProbeResult; the JSONL log records the final attempt's elapsed_ms and a 'detail' that mentions the attempt count when N>1 (e.g. 'ok status=200 (attempt 2/3)').
CLI flags --retry-attempts and --retry-backoff override the defaults; argparse defaults remain the constants above.

tests/test_daily_probe.py covers: (i) connection-refused on first attempt, succeeds on second (passed=True); (ii) all attempts fail (passed=False, detail mentions exhausted retries); (iii) HTTPError 503 retried, then 200 passes.
executor_brief: Add PROBE_RETRY_ATTEMPTS=3 + PROBE_RETRY_BACKOFF_S=1.0 to daily_probe.py constants. Wrap the urlopen call in _execute_probe with a retry loop that catches URLError, TimeoutError, OSError, and HTTPError-outside-accepted; sleep PROBE_RETRY_BACKOFF_S between attempts using time.sleep. Surface the attempt count in the result detail. Add --retry-attempts/--retry-backoff to argparse. Tests must drive every branch — easiest via a counter-based monkeypatch on _execute_probe's urlopen target. Don't add retries to diff_probe.py in this finding's scope.

audit_text: [LOW] No log retention policy. ~/.local/share/bearings-v1/probes/ accumulates one file per day indefinitely. No --max-age-days flag, no logrotate entry, no cleanup note in the install sequence.
spec_anchor: scripts/daily_probe.py:313-346 (_build_arg_parser — arg surface); config/bearings-v1-probe.service (no ExecStartPost cleanup)
code_paths_checked: config/ (no tmpfiles.d, no logrotate.d, no .conf files at all besides the service+timer pair)
verdict_evidence: daily_probe.py argparse exposes only --base-url, --log-dir, --timeout, --quiet. Grep against the script for max-age|max_age|logrotate|retention|cleanup yields no matches. The systemd service has no ExecStartPost cleanup. config/ contains no tmpfiles.d snippet, no logrotate.d entry. The same gap exists for diff_probe.py's diff-probes/ directory. Files therefore accumulate one per day indefinitely under ~/.local/share/bearings-v1/{probes,diff-probes}/.
acceptance_criteria: Both daily_probe.py and diff_probe.py grow a `--max-age-days` flag (default e.g. 30) and a constant PROBE_LOG_RETENTION_DAYS_DEFAULT.

After write_log() succeeds, both scripts call a new prune_old_logs(log_dir, max_age_days, now) helper that deletes files in log_dir matching the YYYY-MM-DD.log pattern whose mtime (or filename-derived date) is older than max_age_days. Pruning errors are non-fatal and logged via the module logger.
Setting --max-age-days=0 disables pruning (explicit opt-out for the dogfood window when Dave wants the full history).

Tests in tests/test_daily_probe.py and tests/test_diff_probe.py cover: pruning across the threshold (file from day -31 deleted, file from day -29 retained), max-age=0 retains everything, prune ignoring non-matching filenames, prune tolerating a missing log_dir.

CHANGELOG.md (or the existing B.1/B.2 sections) documents the new flag and the default retention window. Optionally add a tmpfiles.d snippet under config/ as a belt-and-braces guard, but the in-process prune is the contract.
executor_brief: Add a PROBE_LOG_RETENTION_DAYS_DEFAULT=30 constant and `--max-age-days` argparse flag to both daily_probe.py and diff_probe.py. Implement prune_old_logs(log_dir, max_age_days, now) that scans log_dir for files matching YYYY-MM-DD.log and unlinks ones whose date is more than max_age_days old (skip non-matching names; tolerate missing dir; non-fatal on OSError). Call it after write_log() in both main() flows. Mirror the test patterns already in tests/test_diff_probe.py for write_log; freeze 'now' to make threshold tests deterministic. Setting max-age-days=0 must skip pruning entirely. Flag both scripts in the same commit so the dogfood story stays consistent.
